item.title: US to merge offshore energy regulators split by BP Deepwater Horizon disaster
item.description: Move would reintegrate law enforcement and industrial promotion, which were separated by Obama due to conflicts of interest that led to the worst oil spill in US history

item.title: Wind turbine giant to invest over $10bn in green fuels projects
item.description: Mingyang, a leading manufacturer of wind turbines and other clean power technologies, is yet to comment on announcement from Ethiopian Investment Commission

item.title: France combines offshore wind tenders to create huge auction round
item.description: 10GW round for fixed and floating will reportedly set limits on China-made content in bid to protect European supply chain

item.title: Five key lessons for a data centre and energy win-win
item.description: Big ambitions in AI and data can't succeed if they're out of step with the infrastructure needed to power them, writes Adrian Del Maestro

item.title: Euro MP fears for renewables investments if 'EU patriots' keep lining Trump's pockets
item.description: MEP Nicolás González Casares says key mechanisms such as ETS must be protected as Iran war exposes energy policy fractures

item.title: Mingyang: 'UK has not ruled out our Scottish factory'
item.description: UK government has said Mingyang turbines are not welcome in British offshore wind farms – but supplier insists that is not the final word on its Scottish factory

item.title: Masdar and TotalEnergies to merge wind and solar operations
item.description: UAE green power group and oil giant to create $2.2bn joint venture in Asia

item.title: Data centres just took the football test: for power systems the result was 'profound'
item.description: As grids struggle to cope with demand for connections, a start-up backed by Nvidia, GE Vernova, Siemens and a host of other big names hopes to help rewrite the rules over how massive data centres interact with power networks

item.title: Data centre power took the 'football test': for grids the result was 'profound'
item.description: As grids struggle to cope with demand for connections, a start-up backed by Nvidia, GE Vernova, Siemens and a host of other big names hopes to help rewrite the rules over how massive data centres interact with power networks

item.title: Crown Estate chief talks option fees, wind wakes and GB Energy
item.description: Head of marine at seabed landlord believes new marine route map could be game-changer for leasing round, while discussing more thorny issues

item.title: Crown Estate chief talks option fees, wind wakes and GB Energy
item.description: Head of marine at seabed landlord believes new marine route map could be game-changer for leasing round, while discussing more thorny issues

item.title: Largest US grid faces two key market hurdles as milestone battery tech deploys
item.description: Transmission costs and interconnection barriers in the major US grid continue, but innovations such as RWE's milestone sodium-ion pilot battery could make a difference long-term

item.title: Could new RWE battery be a step to solving storage famine on largest US grid?
item.description: Transmission costs and interconnection barriers in the major US grid continue, but innovations such as RWE's milestone sodium-ion pilot battery could make a difference long-term

item.title: Largest US grid faces two key market hurdles despite RWE's new milestone battery
item.description: Transmission costs and interconnection barriers in the major US grid continue despite RWE's milestone sodium-ion pilot battery

item.title: Ex-Orsted chief: US offshore wind industry's problems 'self inflicted'
item.description: Former onshore CEO says offshore sector tried to play too big a part in power mix too soon

item.title: Big capacity gains and low prices in Germany's booming wind industry
item.description: Tender awarded 3.45GW of new projects on lowest prices since 2018, threatening economics of critical sector

item.title: Once partners, Total and New York now on opposites sides of offshore wind row
item.description: Is $150 per megawatt hour power affordable or not? It may depend on the season

item.title: Trump attacks on offshore wind have had 'terrible' impact, says EU energy chief
item.description: European Union can help 'soften blows' industry has received in US, said bloc’s energy commissioner, where even offshore wind farms nearly built have been targeted by Trump

item.title: Equinor project loses PPA ahead of South Korean offshore wind tender
item.description: Equinor's Bandibuli power purchase agreement is cancelled as Korean government sticks to its guns on offshore wind strategies that include localisation and support for public-led projects

item.title: RWE’s Europe onshore wind head leaves, with successor revealed
item.description: CEO for onshore wind and solar in Europe and Australia announced she's leaving RWE.

item.title: 'Wind has grown up – now Germany's market design needs to catch up'
item.description: The introduction of CfDs needs to build on what has worked while addressing new system realities, writes Catharina Dreier

item.title: Thousands of future wind turbine failures 'could strain supply chain'
item.description: Maintenance capacity could be an increasingly important operational bottleneck over the coming decade, according to a new report

item.title: Is Germany's energy minister a security risk?
item.description: Katherina Reiche is despite war in Middle East insisting on giving gas a greater role in Germany's energy system while pushing for legislation that could slow down energy transition

item.title: Banking giant turns 'firmly positive' on Orsted: 'Trump's Iran war is helping offshore wind'
item.description: Danish group's shares rise as Bank of America says conflict strengthens case in Europe and US risks diminish

item.title: Reader poll: Was UK right to block Mingyang factory?
item.description: With Mingyang’s factory blocked on security grounds, has the UK struck the right balance between investment and risk?

item.title: Cadeler seeks funding for new foundation vessels in tightening market
item.description: Cadeler is pulling no punches in its ambition to provide full-scope solutions, and a bullish view of the European offshore wind market is at the heart of this

item.title: 'Holy sh*t, we're disconnecting': final moments before Spain's blackout
item.description: Recorded calls between grid operators and power producers, reveal the final moments before Spain went dark

item.title: Record offshore wind investments in sight – even as developers grow more selective
item.description: Offshore wind projects with a capacity of close to 16GW will reach final investment decisions this year, according to a new report

item.title: Vestas planning hundreds of job cuts at wind turbine factory
item.description: Danish turbine maker has been in process of cutting its global workforce, which stood at 30,000 last year

item.title: Wind turbine fire that killed three prompts backlash over ageing machines
item.description: Deadly fire and earlier collapse puts age of machines in spotlight in South Korea, but industry expert warns against hasty conclusions
